steps.title: Identify the central problem
steps.content: The passage asks how opposition to lying can be grounded if truthfulness is not an inviolable duty. It then presents Pallikkathayil's solution: false beliefs constrain agency, so respect for agency gives a reason not to lie except in special cases.

steps.title: Reject distorted summaries
steps.content: Choice B shifts the focus to identifying circumstances when lying is permissible. Choice C incorrectly makes the duty inviolable. Choice D says the grounds remain unclear, but the passage offers a possible ground through agency. The answer is A.
